observations: Lines 3-9: the opener alternates medium action sentences with one-word aftermath beats; the cadence matches the action standard.
Lines 15-25: patrol banter stays in short declaratives, with one longer clause only where the political joke needs setup.
Lines 67-71: Sylvanas's road-thought paragraph uses clipped noun-phrase bursts to keep introspection tactical instead of soft.
Lines 71-73: the runner arrival was rhythmically strong but carried one negation-scaffold sentence that softened impact.
Lines 249-263: the identity reveal now pivots from ward-light to classification and command without a softer setup phrase.

fixes.rule: sentenceRhythmStandards.default + emphasisRule — cut negation scaffolding and land on blunt pressure beats
fixes.before: They heard the rider before they saw him. Fast horse. Hard push. Not panic exactly, but close enough to bruise it. Heads turned south together. The patrol had their bows in hand before the runner broke through the trees.
fixes.after: They heard the rider before they saw him. Fast horse. Hard push. Near panic. Close enough to bruise. Heads turned south together. The patrol had their bows in hand before the runner broke through the trees.

fixes.rule: sentenceRhythmStandards.emphasisRule — remove soft setup and let cadence carry the reveal
fixes.before: For a beat there was only ward-light and the old bass note of the stones in the floor.
fixes.after: For a beat: ward-light and the old bass note of the stones in the floor.

observations: Lines 21-29: the narration stays concrete — wrist, shaft, fern frond, trunks, road, papers — and never drifts into planner language.
Lines 55-69: road, hoofbeats, ward-hum, tree spacing, and hollows all keep the lexicon fieldbound and Sylvanas-native.
Lines 141-153: 'theory and papers' is contempt filtered through Sylvanas, not document voice; the line still belongs to her.
Lines 139-157 carried a few low-value adverb/telling residues during the ride into the Pass.
Lines 239-243 read Jaina through boots, burns, calluses, and posture instead of through elegant variation or abstract labels.

fixes.rule: vocabularyStandards.requiredQualities — keep the ride observation concrete and unsugared
fixes.before: Loose gravel replaced the forest road. The mountain wind came down the cut in long steady breaths, carrying stone dust, pine, and the faint iron taste of distant water. The runner stayed half a horse-length ahead and wisely kept silent.
fixes.after: Loose gravel replaced the forest road. The mountain wind came down the cut in long steady breaths, carrying stone dust, pine, and the faint iron taste of distant water. The runner stayed half a horse-length ahead and had sense enough to keep silent.

fixes.rule: vocabularyStandards.baseline — replace the generic adverbial arrival with a harder physical beat
fixes.before: Cyndia was waiting before Sylvanas had fully dismounted.
fixes.after: Cyndia was waiting before Sylvanas hit the ground.

fixes.rule: vocabularyStandards.baseline — remove filler adverb and keep the report plain
fixes.before: Cyndia only nodded. "She's been in holding since. No escape attempt. No demand for envoy privilege. Just the same claim, over and over, until I had her moved out of earshot of the road."
fixes.after: Cyndia nodded. "She's been in holding since. No escape attempt. No demand for envoy privilege. Just the same claim, over and over, until I had her moved out of earshot of the road."

fixes.rule: vocabularyStandards.baseline — replace a weak adverb with a harsher, more physical adjective
fixes.before: The right-hand knuckles were white and pink where cold magic had burned them and healed badly. Calluses, too — not ornamental ones from training yards, but the hard skin of someone who used her staff. She sat upright without leaning back, shoulders rigid, her stare fixed on the confiscated notes.
fixes.after: The right-hand knuckles were white and pink where cold magic had burned them and healed wrong. Calluses, too — not ornamental ones from training yards, but the hard skin of someone who used her staff. She sat upright without leaning back, shoulders rigid, her stare fixed on the confiscated notes.

observations: Line 55: 'The sound of the country's skin holding closed' is a single body-under-strain image grounded in ward maintenance and threat containment.
Line 83: 'The morning narrowed' is brief compression rather than ornament; it sharpens pressure in one stroke.
Line 153: 'It smelled of theory and papers' keeps the figurative move inside Sylvanas's contempt instead of drifting poetic.
Lines 249-253: 'old bass note' and 'ward grid' stay inside military-political and tactile source domains; no mixed metaphor stack appears.
Chapter-wide count is approximately 4 figurative images across 2565 words, or 1.56 per thousand — leaner than the guide's 2-4 band but still tonally appropriate for competence-first opening pressure.
Lines 27-29: the 5-sentence patrol paragraph earns its size because it is active environmental read and social placement, not static exposition.
Lines 67-69: the 5-sentence thought chain also earns its size because Sylvanas is mapping terrain, horses, and ambush habits in one tactical run.
Lines 219-223: the ward-check block was split so the tactile test and the operational conclusion no longer stack into one 9-sentence unit.
Lines 229-231: the room-clear inventory was split into two visual beats, reducing the brick effect without changing content.
Lines 239-243: Jaina's first-look description was split so the physical evidence lands before Sylvanas's judgment follows.

fixes.rule: paragraphLengthGuidelines.typical — break a long fragment stack into two pressure-linked paragraphs
fixes.before: She put a hand to the doorframe, not for balance, but to feel the ward through the stone. Warm. Steady. No violence in it. Whatever the prisoner had done to the network, she had not beaten it. The stones still stood. They had called. Cyndia had answered. The wards had held.
fixes.after:
She put a hand to the doorframe, not for balance, but to feel the ward through the stone. Warm. Steady. No violence in it.

Whatever the prisoner had done to the network, she had not beaten it. The stones still stood. They had called. Cyndia had answered. The wards had held.

fixes.rule: paragraphLengthGuidelines.typical — vary paragraph shape during the room-clear sequence
fixes.before: Her gaze went first where it always went. Exit. Window slit too narrow for use. Bench. Table edge. Staff on the rack. Satchel below it. Ranger to the left of the door, another at the back wall. Then the prisoner.
fixes.after:
Her gaze went first where it always went. Exit. Window slit too narrow for use. Bench. Table edge.

Staff on the rack. Satchel below it. Ranger to the left of the door, another at the back wall. Then the prisoner.

fixes.rule: paragraphLengthGuidelines.sceneBalance — separate evidence gathering from the concluding read on Jaina's body
fixes.before: The woman on the bench had practical road robes dusty at the hem, leather dark at the knees from field use, and boots worn pale across the toes from too many miles in the stirrups. Her braid was trying to come loose. Ink marked two fingers of her left hand. The right-hand knuckles were white and pink where cold magic had burned them and healed badly. Calluses, too — not ornamental ones from training yards, but the hard skin of someone who used her staff. She sat upright without leaning back, shoulders rigid, her stare fixed on the confiscated notes.
fixes.after:
The woman on the bench had practical road robes dusty at the hem, leather dark at the knees from field use, and boots worn pale across the toes from too many miles in the stirrups. Her braid was trying to come loose. Ink marked two fingers of her left hand.

The right-hand knuckles were white and pink where cold magic had burned them and healed wrong. Calluses, too — not ornamental ones from training yards, but the hard skin of someone who used her staff. She sat upright without leaning back, shoulders rigid, her stare fixed on the confiscated notes.

observations: Chapter-wide dialogue-to-narration ratio is 22:78, below the global 45:55 target, but the chapter is intentionally action/travel/briefing heavy and does restore spoken pressure at each decision hinge.
Lines 15-25: the Sylvanas-Lor'themar exchange supplies competence-collision dialogue early, preventing a prose wall after the kill.
Lines 75-121: the runner report and split decision use dialogue for plot turns rather than burying them in summary.
Lines 165-217: Cyndia's briefing stays plain and reportable; the chapter uses 14 no-frills said/asked tags and 0 creative tags.
Line 263: the chapter ends on spoken pressure the moment the scene finally has room to talk.
Lines 3-5: Sylvanas notices the opening under the troll's arm before she notices anything emotional about the kill.
Lines 29-31: she sorts road, Pass, Silvermoon, and paperwork by threat and annoyance rather than by scenery.
Lines 67-69: her attention goes to range, tree spacing, a horse's foreleg, and an eastern hollow — exactly the right tactical filter.
Lines 229-231: she clears the room by exits, obstacles, gear, and personnel before she grants the prisoner visual priority.
Lines 253-257: Jaina becomes a political/security problem in Sylvanas's mind before she becomes a person in need of sympathy.
Adverbs per page after the audit are 1.17, well under the hard cap of 4.
Estimated passive voice is 1.18%, well under the hard cap of 4%.
The chapter uses 1 'like' construction and 0 'as if' constructions, so comparison fog never takes over.
Filter-word density is low; the chapter generally reports sensation directly instead of routing it through noticed/felt/saw scaffolding.
Word count moved from 2569 to 2565 in the audit pass, preserving the no-expansion rule while tightening filler.

fixes.rule: proseQualityFloor.showTellRatioTarget — strip filler so the report lands more directly
fixes.before: Cyndia only nodded. "She's been in holding since. No escape attempt. No demand for envoy privilege. Just the same claim, over and over, until I had her moved out of earshot of the road."
fixes.after: Cyndia nodded. "She's been in holding since. No escape attempt. No demand for envoy privilege. Just the same claim, over and over, until I had her moved out of earshot of the road."

observations: Lines 27-29: horses, wards, and long-road routine arrive through patrol behavior, not through a worldbuilding stop.
Lines 55-56: the ward-hum is explained by teeth and tongue pressure, which keeps the magical fact bodily and local.
Lines 81-101: the human-mage problem enters through a runner's clipped report and Sylvanas's suspicion, not a narrator aside on inter-kingdom politics.
Lines 165-195: Cyndia gives only tactical consequence and anomaly shape; she does not gloss leyline theory for its own sake.
Lines 247-253: Jaina's political significance arrives through reports, bloodline, and location inside the ward grid, not through detached lore summary.
Lines 75-81: the runner kneels and reports in clipped units, establishing Sylvanas's institutional dominance immediately.
Lines 93-107: Sylvanas controls the questioning through short prompts; the runner answers and never seizes sentence length.
Lines 163-195: Cyndia's report is crisp but subordinate, which fits competent hierarchy rather than noisy deference.
Lines 247-257: the identity reveal tightens Sylvanas's syntax into classifications, then into the imperative 'Start talking.'
Lines 85-129: Lor'themar is the one real near-equal in the chapter; the matched dry exchanges show parity without erasing Sylvanas's command position.
Lines 3-25: the opening gambit is in-medias-res action followed by friction-laced banter, which matches the guide.
Lines 55-71: the easy road sequence establishes the comfort state that the chapter is about to violate.
Lines 71-121: the runner arrival lands near the middle and changes the governing question from patrol routine to ward breach.
Lines 155-217: the arrival at the post narrows options through briefing and custody detail instead of replaying the same report.
Lines 247-263: the chapter resolves as cliff-edge forward pressure rather than as explanation, which is the correct chapter-closing shape.
Lines 55-56: the ward-hum reads as familiar national infrastructure, not as spectacle.
Line 81: the leyline claim enters as a problem report; the prose treats it as a breach variable, not as a magic showpiece.
Lines 135-139: the closer-spaced stones are felt through pulses, rein pressure, and mountain air, which keeps magic domesticated.
Lines 185-195: Cyndia describes stone behavior by reaction and failure mode, which fits the style guide's consequence-first rule.
Lines 219-221: Sylvanas checks ward warmth and steadiness like a commander checking a wall, with awe completely withheld.
Lines 15-23: the budget joke is really an argument about patrol staffing, competence, and institutional stupidity.
Lines 49-53: the Cyndia exchange is nominally logistical, but the subtext is that everyone knows Sylvanas's habits better than she likes.
Lines 59-63: the bridge-repair jokes are really contempt for the Convocation's priorities.
Lines 85-91: Lor'themar's remark about the wasted horse carries shared history and mild accusation without saying either outright.
Lines 127-129: the parting joke trades operational concern through wit instead of direct sentiment.
Lines 3-5: the kill beat uses short impact sentences and one longer geometry sentence to keep action legible.
Lines 71-73: the runner arrival uses micro-sentences at the right tension spike and does not stay clipped long enough to turn monotone.
Lines 133-155: the ride south compresses travel and only expands the details that change threat assessment.
Lines 229-231: the room-clear fragments prioritize sightlines and bodies over decorative description, which fits close-tension pacing.
Lines 261-263: time dilates again only at the interrogation threshold, exactly where the scene gains a new governing risk.
Lines 15-19: Lor'themar's budget line is deadpan understatement, not quippy riffing.
Line 23: Sylvanas's answer uses contempt to needle the Convocation, which matches her humor fingerprint.
Lines 61-63: the 'ancient principle' line stays in-world and political; it does not wink at the reader.
Lines 85-91: 'waste a horse proving it' is tired insider wit under pressure, not comic-relief drift.
Lines 127-129: the last joke lands before the holding room and then humor disappears, which matches the chapter's tonal drop.

fingerprintChecks.vocabularyLevel: pass — lines 21-29, 55-69, and 229-257 stay lean, martial, and concrete; the audit removed the few leftover soft adverbial beats.
fingerprintChecks.sentenceRhythm: pass — short declaratives dominate, with longer clauses only when terrain or political implication needs mapping; lines 71 and 249 were tightened to keep the cadence hard.
fingerprintChecks.metaphorDensity: pass — figurative language stays sparse and draws from bodies, pressure, and containment; density remains lean rather than ornate.
fingerprintChecks.emotionalRegister: pass — emotion shows as irritation, classification, pressure, and command rather than open naming; even Jaina's reveal becomes a security problem first.
fingerprintChecks.thoughtPatterns: pass — lines 141-153 and 253-257 follow threat assessment -> utility/danger judgment -> decision, exactly as specified.
fingerprintChecks.sensorySignature: pass — the chapter privileges footfall, hoofbeats, ward pressure, exits, distance, posture, and exposed ground over decorative color.

ruleViolations: Line 71 originally used a negation-scaffold pressure beat that softened Sylvanas's harder rhythm; fixed in v4b.
Line 139 originally leaned on the adverb 'wisely' instead of trusting Sylvanas's sharper contempt; fixed in v4b.
Line 157 originally used the softer 'fully dismounted' phrasing instead of a harder physical arrival beat; fixed in v4b.
Line 191 originally carried the filler phrase 'only nodded'; fixed in v4b.
Line 241 originally ended the burn read on 'healed badly' rather than the harsher 'healed wrong'; fixed in v4b.
Line 249 originally used 'there was only' instead of landing the reveal in Sylvanas's harder cadence; fixed in v4b.
